

Aragón‐Parada, J., Carrillo‐Reyes, P., Rodríguez, A., Munguía‐Lino, G.,
Salinas‐Rodríguez, M.M. & De‐Nova, J.A. (2023) [Spatial phylogenetics of
the flora in the sierra madre del sur, mexico: Evolutionary puzzles in
tropical mountains](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.14679). *Journal of
Biogeography*, **50**, 1679--1691.

Dlamini, W.M.D. & Loffler, L. (2023) *[Tree species diversity and
richness patterns reveal high priority areas for conservation in
eswatini](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-981-99-0131-9_8)*. *Ecosystem and
species habitat modeling for conservation and restoration*, pp.
141--167. Springer Nature Singapore.

Erst, A.S., Baasanmunkh, S., Tsegmed, Z., Oyundelger, K., Sharples,
M.T., Oyuntsetseg, B., Krivenko, D.A., Gureyeva, I.I., Romanets, R.R.,
Kuznetsov, A.A., Kechaykin, A.A., Shmakov, A.I., Maltseva, S.Yu., Erst,
T.V., Wang, W., Gil, H.-Y. & Choi, H.J. (2023) [Hotspot and conservation
gap analysis of endemic vascular plants in the altai mountain country
based on a new global conservation
assessment](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.gecco.2023.e02647). *Global
Ecology and Conservation*, **47**, 02647.

Fernandes, N.B.G., Moraes, A.M. & Milward-de-Azevedo, M.A. (2023)
[Diversity of the passiflora l. In the serra do mar ecoregion and the
relationships with environmental gradients, south and southeast,
brazil](https://doi.org/10.1590/1677-941x-abb-2022-0314). *Acta Botanica
Brasilica*, **37**.

Flores-Argüelles, A., López-Ferrari, A.R. & Espejo-Serna, A. (2023)
[Geographic distribution and endemism of bromeliaceae from the western
sierra-coast region of jalisco,
mexico](https://doi.org/10.17129/botsci.3169). *Botanical Sciences*,
**101**, 527--543.

Flores-Tolentino, M., Ramírez-Rodríguez, J.R., Morales-Linares, J.,
Ibarra-Manríquez, G., Dorado, Ó. & Villaseñor, J.L. (2023) [Delimitación
geográfica y florística de la provincia fisiográfica de la depresión del
balsas, méxico, con énfasis en el bosque tropical estacionalmente
seco](https://doi.org/10.22201/ib.20078706e.2023.94.4985). *Revista
Mexicana de Biodiversidad*, **94**, 944985.

Francisco-Gutiérrez, A., Ruiz-Sanchez, E. & Lira-Noriega, A. (2023)
[Biogeografía y evaluaciones de conservación de las especies de
lamourouxia (orobanchaceae)](https://doi.org/10.21829/abm130.2023.2213).
*Acta Botanica Mexicana*.

González-Orozco, C.E., Diaz-Giraldo, R.A. & Rodriguez-Castañeda, C.
(2023) [An early warning for better planning of agricultural expansion
and biodiversity conservation in the orinoco high plains of
colombia](https://doi.org/10.3389/fsufs.2023.1192054). *Frontiers in
Sustainable Food Systems*, **7**.

González‐Orozco, C.E., Reyes‐Herrera, P.H., Sosa, C.C., Torres, R.T.,
Manrique‐Carpintero, N.C., Lasso‐Paredes, Z., Cerón‐Souza, I. &
Yockteng, R. (2023) [Wild relatives of potato (solanum l. Sec. Petota)
poorly sampled and unprotected in
colombia](https://doi.org/10.1002/csc2.21143). *Crop Science*, **64**,
225--243.

Guo, W.-Y., Serra-Diaz, J.M., Eiserhardt, W.L., Maitner, B.S., Merow,
C., Violle, C., Pound, M.J., Sun, M., Slik, F., Blach-Overgaard, A.,
Enquist, B.J. & Svenning, J.-C. (2023) [Climate change and land use
threaten global hotspots of phylogenetic endemism for
trees](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-023-42671-y). *Nature
Communications*, **14**.

Mardones, D. & Scherson, R.A. (2023) [Hotspots within a hotspot:
Evolutionary measures unveil interesting biogeographic patterns in
threatened coastal forests in
chile](https://doi.org/10.1093/botlinnean/boad002). *Botanical Journal
of the Linnean Society*, **202**, 433--448.

McCurry, M.R., Park, T., Coombs, E.J., Hart, L.J. & Laffan, S. (2023)
[Latitudinal gradients in the skull shape and assemblage structure of
delphinoid cetaceans](https://doi.org/10.1093/biolinnean/blac128).
*Biological Journal of the Linnean Society*, **138**, 470--480.

Miller, J.T., Prentice, E., Bui, E.N., Knerr, N., Mishler, B.D.,
Schmidt‐Lebuhn, A.N., González‐Orozco, C. & Laffan, S. (2023) [Banksia
(proteaceae) contains less phylogenetic diversity than expected in
southwestern australia](https://doi.org/10.1111/jse.13019). *Journal of
Systematics and Evolution*, **61**, 957--966.

Molina-Paniagua, M.E., Alves de Melo, P.H., Ramírez-Barahona, S., Monro,
A.K., Burelo-Ramos, C.M., Gómez-Domínguez, H. & Ortiz-Rodriguez, A.E.
(2023) [How diverse are the mountain karst forests of
mexico?](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0292352) *PLOS ONE*,
**18**, 0292352.

Nicolau, G.K. & Edwards, S. (2023) [Diversity and endemism of southern
african gekkonids linked with the escarpment has implications for
conservation priorities](https://doi.org/10.3390/d15020306).
*Diversity*, **15**, 306.

Ortiz-Brunel, J.P., Ochoterena, H., Moore, M.J., Aragón-Parada, J.,
Flores, J., Munguía-Lino, G., Rodríguez, A., Salinas-Rodríguez, M.M. &
Flores-Olvera, H. (2023) [Patterns of richness and endemism in the
gypsicolous flora of mexico](https://doi.org/10.3390/d15040522).
*Diversity*, **15**, 522.

Pedro, D. de, Ceccarelli, F.S., Vandame, R., Mérida, J. & Sagot, P.
(2023) [Congruence between species richness and phylogenetic diversity
in north america for the bee genus diadasia (hymenoptera:
apidae)](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10531-023-02706-8). *Biodiversity and
Conservation*, **32**, 4445--4459.

Ramírez-Verdugo, P., Tapia, A., Forest, F. & Scherson, R.A. (2023)
[Evolutionary diversity of the endemic genera of the vascular flora of
chile and its implications for
conservation](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0287957). *PLOS ONE*,
**18**, 0287957.

RUIZ-SANCHEZ, E., MUNGUÍA-LINO, G., PIANISSOLA, E.M., ELY, F. & CLARK,
L.G. (2023) [Richness and endemism in chusquea subg. Swallenochloa
(poaceae), a neotropical subgenus adapted to temperate
conditions](https://doi.org/10.11646/phytotaxa.609.3.2). *Phytotaxa*,
**609**, 180--194.

Villaseñor, J.L., Ortiz, E. & Hernández-Flores, M.M. (2023) [The
vascular plant species endemic or nearly endemic to puebla,
mexico](https://doi.org/10.17129/botsci.3299). *Botanical Sciences*,
**101**, 1207--1221.

Volis, S. & Tojibaev, K. (2023) [Comparison of maps of species richness
and endemism produced directly from species occurrences with those
produced by stacked species distribution
modelling](https://doi.org/10.1163/22244662-bja10072). *Israel Journal
of Ecology and Evolution*, 1--10.

Wang, C., Zhu, S., Jiang, X., Chen, S., Xiao, Y., Zhao, Y., Yan, Y. &
Wen, Y. (2023) [Spatio-temporal variation of species richness and
phylogenetic diversity patterns for spring ephemeral plants in northern
china](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.gecco.2023.e02752). *Global Ecology and
Conservation*, **48**, 02752.

Wang, G., Scherson, R.A., Vera, D., Bai, Y., Wen, J., Guo, L., Hughes,
A.C. & Wang, H. (2023) [Spatial patterns and drivers of native plant
diversity in hainan, china](https://doi.org/10.1111/jse.13017). *Journal
of Systematics and Evolution*, **62**, 603--620.

Ye, C., Liu, H., Qin, H., Shu, J., Zhou, Z. & Jin, X. (2023)
[Geographical distribution and conservation strategy of national key
protected wild plants of
china](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.isci.2023.107364). *iScience*, **26**,
107364.

Zhang, H., Chen, S., Bonser, S.P., Hitchcock, T. & Moles, A.T. (2023)
[Factors that shape large‐scale gradients in
clonality](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.14577). *Journal of
Biogeography*, **50**, 827--837.

Zhou, R., Ci, X., Hu, J., Zhang, X., Cao, G., Xiao, J., Liu, Z., Li, L.,
Thornhill, A.H., Conran, J.G. & Li, J. (2023) [Transitional areas of
vegetation as biodiversity hotspots evidenced by multifaceted
biodiversity analysis of a dominant group in chinese evergreen
broad-leaved forests](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolind.2023.110001).
*Ecological Indicators*, **147**, 110001.
